title: State-of-Charge Cycling Window Dependence of Lithium-Ion Battery Degradation Under Real-World BEV Fast Charging
authors: Sankaranarayanan Venkatakrishnan and Ramaraja P. Ramasamy
abstract: This study investigates how different state‑of‑charge cycling windows under a realistic fast‑charging profile affect the performance and aging of nickel cobalt aluminum oxide-graphite 18650 lithium‑ion cells. The study employs a scaled-down 150 kW battery electric vehicle fast‑charging profile in which peak power is applied only over a narrow 10%–40% SoC interval before tapering, in contrast to conventional 0%–100% constant current—constant voltage charging. Cycling tests cover partial (0%–50%, 0%–80%) and full (0%–100%) state-of-charge windows, together with a full‑cycling constant current—co…

title: Early Prediction of Lithium-Ion Battery Lifetime with Random Charge-Discharge Behaviors and Feature Noise
authors: Guisong Wang, Jie Liu, Cong Wang and Yunxia Chen
abstract: The accuracy of lithium-ion battery lifetime prediction heavily relies on high-quality data, which typically exhibits complete charge-discharge cycles and low feature noise. However, accurate lifetime prediction faces significant challenges when batteries are subject to random charge-discharge behaviors and intrinsic feature noise. To address this issue, this paper proposes an early prediction framework suitable for such conditions. The proposed approach incorporates an adaptive variable window moving average (AVWMA) filter, which utilizes change-point analysis (CPA), along with a dynamic wind…
